focus: RCBC (SI #47) D4 path active. DC#2 education confirmed. DC#3 healthcare next.

SI45_CIAP.ontological_types: theoretical (DC#1 game theory), designed empirical (DC#2 adversarial ML), emergent competitive (DC#3 economic markets)
SI45_CIAP.note: Highest-confidence D4 theorem. Meta-theorem: ceiling_TCA and ceiling_GCRSC are CIAP corollaries.

SI47_RCBC.description: RAAI × CIAP conjunction: researchers optimizing training pipelines against fixed benchmarks face CIAP-fails (Goodhart at meta level), while RAAI/AATE ensures trained models cannot introspectively detect benchmark-CA divergence. Double-layer CA-degradation predicts benchmark score inflation while CA stagnates/degrades.
SI47_RCBC.derivation_1: RAAI-DPI × CIAP-fails: double-DPI chain. CA(X) → B_optimized(X) → θ → Î(X) Markov chain. Two chained information inequalities compound.
SI47_RCBC.derivation_2: AATE × CIAP: SDT d-prime instrument degradation path. AATE: d'(n)→0 under OA-feedback training. CIAP-fails: super-linear acceleration of instrument degradation under adaptive optimization pressure. Independent of RAAI-DPI.
SI47_RCBC.dc1: AI benchmark saturation literature: MMLU saturation, BIG-Bench novel vs. known, Chatbot Arena vs. structured tasks, capability overhang pattern — 4 independent evidence programs. Ontological type: designed empirical.
SI47_RCBC.dc2: STEM education benchmarks / NCLB teaching-to-the-test: NAEP vs. state assessment divergence (Haney 2000), Koretz score inflation studies, Campbell's Law gaming evidence (Jacob & Levitt 2003, Cullen & Reback 2006), PISA/TIMSS international comparison stability — 4 independent evidence programs. Ontological type: emergent social system.
SI47_RCBC.dc3_candidate: Healthcare quality scorecard gaming (hospital CMS star ratings, Leapfrog, Joint Commission). Third ontological type: regulated institutional domain.
SI47_RCBC.d4_path: ZR cycle 1 complete (passive, C76). ZR cycle 2 in progress (C77+). Need DC#3 + 1 more ZR cycle. Target confidence 0.82+.
SI47_RCBC.note: First second-order conjunction theorem in framework — derives from two D4 theorems (RAAI, CIAP/AATE) jointly applied. Ontological type coverage: designed empirical (AI) + emergent social (education) → next: regulated institutional (healthcare).

key_insight_c77: RCBC DC#2 confirms cross-ontological-type generality. The CIAP-fails + RAAI-analog mechanism operates identically in designed-empirical systems (AI benchmarks, DC#1) and emergent social systems (NCLB education accountability, DC#2). The ontological type of the measurement system does not matter — what matters is the structural combination of: (1) fixed metric + adaptive optimization agent, and (2) no internal CA-corrective signal. This is important for the framework's generality claim: RCBC is not a claim about AI specifically, it is a claim about metric-optimization dynamics generally.

dc2_evidence_structure.E1: NAEP vs. state assessment divergence — independent measurement instrument shows smaller gains than optimized-against instrument
dc2_evidence_structure.E2: Koretz parallel-test studies — direct experimental isolation of score inflation vs. genuine CA improvement
dc2_evidence_structure.E3: Campbell's Law gaming documentation — reclassification, cheating, curriculum narrowing — adaptive strategies inflating metric without CA improvement
dc2_evidence_structure.E4: PISA/TIMSS international comparison — non-optimized-against international assessments flat while domestic high-stakes tests rose

theorem_tier_structure.tier_2: AIIP, AATE, AAA, KDA, KCRIT, ETD, MCCF, CIAP, RAAI — derived from K1-K7
theorem_tier_structure.tier_3_new: RCBC — derived from two D4 tier-2 theorems (RAAI + CIAP/AATE) in conjunction

passive_accumulation.RAAI_ZR: D4 confirmed — ZR now D4-maintenance mode
passive_accumulation.CIAP_ZR: D4 confirmed — ZR now D4-maintenance mode
